 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","title":"Install section with three tabs (agent paste / jsm CLI / manual ZIP)","description":"The single most important section of the article: the non-technical install flow. This is the payoff of the entire auth-code design.

## Section structure (id="install")
SectionHeader eyebrow="Install" title="Get the skill into your agent in 90 seconds."

Then three labeled tabs/cards (Id lean toward 3 stacked cards on mobile and a tab group on desktop — build it as 3 cards with subtle emphasis on card #1):

### Card 1: "In Claude or Codex Desktop (recommended, no terminal)" — default/emphasized

1. **Short pitch**: If youre not a developer, this is the only install path you need. You already have Claude Desktop or Codex Desktop installed (if not, link to the download pages). You have a paid jeffreys-skills.md subscription. Thats it.

2. **Step 1**: Visit the skill page on jeffreys-skills.md (link). Make sure youre signed in.

3. **Step 2**: Click `Generate install prompt`. The site mints a one-time, 10-minute-TTL, single-use code tied to your account and gives you a small block of text to copy.

4. **Step 3**: Open Claude or Codex Desktop. Paste the text. The agent reads it, asks you once to approve the single download tool call (standard "allow this command?" dialog), downloads the bundle, extracts it to the right folder, and tells you to reload skills.

5. **Step 4**: The skill appears in the skill picker. You type `/wills` or just describe what you want (`"I want to review my existing will — I moved from CA to TX last year"`) and the agent routes through the skill.

**Callout**: `Why this works without a terminal` — "The agent IS the terminal. You never type a command; you approve one. The URL embedded in the prompt is a single-use token bound to your paid session, so even if you paste the prompt into someone elses computer it wont work there: the code expires before it can be reused."

### Card 2: "Via the jsm CLI (for developers)"
- Same as slack articles pattern: install jsm, login, `jsm install wills-and-estate-planning-skill`.
- Also mention `claude plugins install` alternative if/when that channel is supported.
- Keep tight — this is the familiar path.

### Card 3: "Manual download (fallback)"
- When the agent-paste flow doesnt work (e.g. the user is running Claude in Chrome or a constrained environment), they can click `Download bundle (.zip)` on the skill page. Same single-use code under the hood.
- Instructions: unzip, then drag `wills-and-estate-planning-skill/` into `~/.claude/skills/` (macOS/Linux) or `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\skills\` (Windows). Same for `~/.codex/skills/` or `~/.agents/skills/` if using Codex.
- Brief mention that theres a "reveal folder" helper button on the skill page that opens Finder/Explorer pointing at the right location.

## Pedagogical aside
After card 3, a short explanatory paragraph about the auth-code flow: the signed URL is how jeffreys-skills.md proves to itself that the paid subscriber clicked the button. Its the same pattern as `jsm login` that developers already know, just re-aimed at install.

## Dependency / coordination
This sections copy should mention specific URLs like `jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill`. The jsm.md-side beads need to have the install-card UI live before this sections instructions are technically accurate. Acceptable to ship the article with the UI partially live and update in a follow-up PR.

## Acceptance criteria
- Three cards render correctly on mobile and desktop.
- Each card has a clear CTA pointing at jsm.md.
- No mock code blocks that imply the user must type bash — the agent does everything.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:30.447308428Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:57:07.466443691Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:30.447308428Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:16.260450848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:12.714423898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW 2026-04-18: Two strengthenings needed.

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.12","title":"'Who this is for' section with tier-triage visualization","description":"Address the question every reader has after the install section: "does this apply to me?"

## Section structure (id="who")
SectionHeader eyebrow="Scope" title="Who this is for (and who it isnt)."

### Opening paragraph
Most people think estate planning is for rich people. It is not. Its for anyone who owns anything, cares about anyone, or would prefer a county court not decide what happens to their kids. The skill scales from Tier 1 (renter, mid-20s, no dependents) through Tier 5 ($100M+ industrialist with a private foundation), and the same interview adapts itself to whichever tier matches your facts.

### Embed the TierTriageViz visualization
(Visualization implemented in a separate bead — `wills-estate-visualizations.tsx`.)

Interactive: five rungs of a wealth ladder, each with:
- Net worth band
- Archetype ("young-family renter", "suburban homeowner + 401(k)", "founder with RSUs + SLAT candidate", "industrialist / dynasty")
- Primary goals (guardianship, probate avoidance, transfer tax minimization, family governance)
- Core deliverables (will, POAs, beneficiary audit → revocable trust + QTIP → GRAT + IDGT + dynasty trust, etc.)

Tapping a rung expands an inline detail panel. Default selection should be Tier 2 (middle-class homeowner), since thats the modal reader.

### Complexity overlays
Below the viz, a short list of "what bumps you up a tier regardless of net worth":
- Blended family (+1)
- Non-citizen spouse (+1, QDOT territory)
- Privately held business (+1)
- Disabled or vulnerable heir requiring SNT (+1)
- Multi-state or multi-country assets (+1)
- Specialty items: firearms (NFA trust), crypto self-custody, creator-economy IP, pre-IPO stock, addiction/capacity concerns.

### Who this is NOT for (honest scope limits)
- Non-U.S. domiciliaries. The skill is U.S. federal + state + local; foreign-asset overlays exist but the core is U.S. law.
- People who need a will *today* (bedside signing). The skill has an `urgent-bedside-signing` mode, but for true emergencies you want a lawyer on the phone, not an agent.
- People who want a single-page "just write my will" interaction. The skill is deliberately thorough — it produces 45 artifacts — because coherence across documents is the whole point. If you just want a $30 LegalZoom will, go buy one.

## Acceptance criteria
- Visualization renders and is keyboard-accessible.
- Scope limits are explicit — reduces complaints post-launch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:52.573101216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:24:29.193853884Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:24:29.190773721Z","close_reason":"Who-this-is-for section fully implemented in wills-estate-article.tsx lines 886-1007 with TierTriageViz wired in at line 905","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.12","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:52.573101216Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.12","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.21","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:14.384369815Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.12","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:12.838960023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.13","title":"Core insight section: Axiom 0 + Axiom 1, one coherent story","description":"The thesis section. This is the part that, if the reader leaves with nothing else, should still change how they think about estate planning.

## Section structure (id="insight")
SectionHeader eyebrow="The thesis" title="Why a will alone is not the plan."

### Narrative beats

1. **The most common failure mode is not a drafting error; it is incoherence across documents.** A beautifully-drafted will that says "everything to my kids" does nothing if the 401(k) still names an ex-spouse, because retirement accounts pass by *contract*, not by will. Axiom 0: the will does not control the whole estate.

2. **The plan is the whole story.** Will + revocable trust + beneficiary forms + deeds + POAs + healthcare directives + letter of instruction + digital inventory must all point at the same people, in the same shares, under the same contingencies, under the same states law. Axiom 1: one coherent story.

3. **Concrete example (drive it home)**: "Imagine you die tonight. Your spouse has to locate and call eight institutions in seven days: your 401(k) administrator, two IRA custodians, your life-insurance company, your countys register of deeds, your bank, your brokerage, and the guardian you named for your kids. Each of them will ask a different question. Each of them will accept a different document as proof. The skills job is to make sure the answer they get, and the document you produced, match — so your spouse doesnt spend six months learning probate law at the worst possible time."

### Embed the AxiomCoherenceViz visualization
An interactive SVG/D3 diagram showing:
- Center node: "You"
- 8 spokes out to asset/decision categories (house, 401k, IRA, life insurance, brokerage, POAs, healthcare, digital).
- Each spoke shows which document controls that asset (will / trust / beneficiary form / titling / POA / etc.).
- Toggle "incoherence mode" that highlights mismatches — e.g., a will saying "to kids" but the 401(k) still naming the ex-spouse turns that spoke red.
- Interactive: click a spoke, read a sentence-long plain-English explanation.

### Reinforce with 3 more axioms
- **Axiom 2**: Plan for incapacity first, death second. Dementia, stroke, and coma damage more wealth than death, and lasts longer.
- **Axiom 5**: Under the $15M exemption (2026 OBBBA), basis often dominates tax. Most families are better off *not* gifting appreciated assets out of the estate.
- **Axiom 10**: Communication is the actual work. Surprise at the reading of the will is one of the fastest ways to turn a plan into litigation.

(The other 7 axioms live in the primer and SKILL.md — dont try to cover all 11 here; the article is not a replacement for the skill.)

## Tone discipline
- Every technical term should have a `<J>` tooltip.
- Use names and stakes. Not "elective share." "The rule that says your spouse can override your will and take roughly a third no matter what your will says."
- No code.

## Acceptance criteria
- Visualization mounts, works on touch devices, respects reduced-motion.
- At least one reader test (a non-technical friend/family member) should read this section and be able to articulate "the will is not the plan" back to you.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:38:16.997426357Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:14.524588227Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.13","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:38:16.997426357Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.13","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:14.523855615Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.13","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:12.961771157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.14","title":"Sample session walkthrough (blended-family HNW archetype with IntakePhasesViz)","description":"The emotional centerpiece. A narrated sample session that makes the 9 intake phases concrete.

## Archetype chosen
A **Tier 3 HNW blended family** — because it hits the most instructive patterns in one session:
- Second marriage (both spouses have first-marriage kids)
- One adult child from first marriage is in active addiction recovery
- Primary residence in NY (state estate tax!), vacation home in FL
- One spouse has a pre-IPO startup stake (concentrated position + liquidity risk)
- Family parrot (longer life expectancy than many humans — ties back to the hero illustration and the X-post hook)
- Modest crypto self-custody
- Previous will from 2018 (pre-OBBBA, pre-Secure Act, pre-remarriage)

This archetype cleanly triggers: elective-share, QTIP, vulnerable-heir SNT or incentive trust, NY-FL domicile analysis, liquidity-at-death operator, digital-asset inventory, pet-trust.

## Section structure (id="intake")
SectionHeader eyebrow="A sample session" title="What it actually feels like."

### Presentation
- Narrated from the users perspective ("Maya, 54, remarrying for the second time").
- 9 phases laid out as scenes. For each phase, show:
  - The agents conversational prompt (plain English, not forms).
  - Mayas answer (excerpted / paraphrased).
  - The artifact the agent quietly produces (e.g., `intake-record.md` appended).
  - Any landmine the agent flags as it goes (e.g., "Your ex-husband is still the primary beneficiary on the old 401(k). Flagging `red-flag-triage.md`.").
- After scene 5 (family dynamics), the agent triggers the `blended-family` overlay and the tone of the questions deepens.

### Embed IntakePhasesViz
Visualization showing all 9 phases as a horizontal flow (Orientation → People → Assets → Beneficiary Audit → Family Dynamics → Goals & Values → Incapacity → Jurisdiction → Tier Routing). Each node:
- Clickable — drills into what that phase asks
- Highlight when the narrative is "in" that phase
- Shows approximate time spent (5/10/15 min as in SKILL.md)

### Close the scene
- End with Maya getting a Comprehensive Plan Report, an Attorney Engagement Brief, and a 30-item Implementation Ledger — all produced across a weekend, rather than six months of attorney email threads.
- Crucial line: "The attorney she eventually hires doesnt look at this packet and say a robot did your planning. She looks at it and says good — youve done my diligence work. I can draft in three hours instead of fifteen, and Ill charge you for three."

## Why this archetype
The slack articles emotional centerpiece was the cutover moment. The wills article needs an equivalent — and the blended-family + addicted-child + parrot + crypto combo is vivid enough to stick. Avoid making the archetype so rich that no reader identifies with it; keep one or two elements (the remarriage, the kids) universal.

## Acceptance criteria
- Reads like narrative, not documentation.
- Every `<J>` tooltip works.
- IntakePhasesViz phase-linking animates smoothly as the reader scrolls through the scenes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:38:42.043211696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:57:15.994050188Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.14","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:38:42.043211696Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.14","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.23","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:14.657149425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.14","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:13.086423399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":5,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.14","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW 2026-04-18: IntakePhasesViz scroll-linking (hgjp.23) needs DOM hooks on narrative paragraphs.  {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.15","title":"What the skill produces: deliverables section + DeliverablesTreeViz","description":"Concrete deliverables inventory section — tangible proof that "200 files" is real work, not marketing.

## Section structure (id="produces")
SectionHeader eyebrow="The output" title="The 45 artifacts you walk away with."

### Intro
Every serious session produces a structured project directory, not a pile of loose files. The skill enforces a coverage matrix (`analyses/plan-coverage-matrix.md`) and runs `scripts/plan-validator.py` as a backstop to catch untouched starter outputs. What you end up with, on a weekend afternoon, is something a law firm would charge $8-25k to produce in a month.

### Embed DeliverablesTreeViz
Interactive folder tree:
- `my-estate-plan/`
  - `intake/` (2 files)
    - `intake-record.md`
    - `session-NN-summary.md`
  - `analyses/` (20 files) — `current-document-audit`, `beneficiary-form-audit`, `titling-audit`, `coherence-audit`, `tax-exposure-analysis`, `liquidity-analysis`, `prior-plan-gap-analysis`, `decision-ledger`, `official-source-log`, `red-flag-triage`, `document-acquisition-plan`, `evidence-confidence-map`, `stress-test-scenarios`, `plan-coverage-matrix`, `document-quality-triage`, `recommendation-confidence-register`, `fiduciary-bench-scorecard`, `litigation-risk-memo`, `attorney-handoff-readiness`, `foreign-and-conflict-of-laws-review`.
  - `deliverables/` (23 files) — `asset-inventory`, `beneficiary-map`, `plan-report`, `implementation-ledger`, `letter-of-instruction`, `digital-inventory`, `personal-property-memorandum`, `letter-of-wishes`, `ethical-will`, `family-meeting-agenda`, `conflict-prevention-plan`, `if-i-die-tomorrow`, `disposition-of-remains`, `executor-checklist`, `attorney-interview-questions`, `attorney-engagement-brief`, `document-package-index`, `review-schedule`, `signing-readiness-checklist`, `funding-proof-log`, `institution-contact-matrix`, `beneficiary-change-packet`, `business-continuity-activation`.

Each leaf:
- Click reveals: a 2-sentence plain-English description + a representative output snippet (from the skills `assets/` templates).
- Color-coded by type: intake = gray, analyses = blue, deliverables = emerald.

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.16","title":"9-step workflow narrative section","description":"Section showing the 9-step end-to-end workflow as an explicit flow — counterweight to the intake section (which is 1-on-1 dialogue). This one is the skills macro structure.

## Section structure (id="workflow")
SectionHeader eyebrow="End-to-end" title="The 9 steps, in order."

### Content
Walk through the 9 steps from SKILL.md, each with a 2-3 sentence description in plain English:

1. **Orient** — Why now? What triggered this? (A move, a birth, a divorce, a diagnosis, a scare.) Set the disclaimer; confirm "this is not legal advice."
2. **Inventory** — People + assets + beneficiary audit. Produce the Asset Inventory, Beneficiary Map, Evidence Confidence Map.
3. **Surface complications** — Blended family? Addiction? Disability? Ex-spouse still on a form? This is where the agent names the landmines.
4. **Clarify goals** — Equal vs. equitable. Charitable intent. Business succession. Write these in your own words (they go into the letter of wishes later).
5. **Design structure** — Apply the kernel + operators + tier logic. Pick will + trust + POA architecture. Name executor, trustee, guardian, agents.
6. **Incapacity & end-of-life** — Durable POA, healthcare proxy, living will, HIPAA, POLST if appropriate, disposition of remains.
7. **Tax & domicile** — Federal exemption + state estate / inheritance tax + portability + basis planning. Domicile audit if multi-state.
8. **Communication & legacy** — Family meeting agenda, letter of wishes, ethical will, digital inventory, conflict-prevention plan, "if I die tomorrow" package.
9. **Attorney handoff** — The Comprehensive Plan Report + Attorney Engagement Brief + Interview Questions. Execution checklist. Review cadence.

### Visualization idea (may piggyback on IntakePhasesViz)
If the viz in the sample-session section is already designed to show *phases* (the conversational side), this workflow section could use a simpler horizontal step-bar indicator, or we reuse the same component in a different mode. Decide during implementation; avoid shipping two redundant flow visualizations.

### Key line to land
"Intake (the 9 phases) is what you feel while using the skill. The workflow (the 9 steps) is what the agent and the skill produce for you in parallel. By the end of phase 9, step 9 is also done, and you hand the whole packet to a lawyer."

## Acceptance criteria
- Reads as a natural follow-up to the sample-session section.
- Doesnt repeat the intake-phases content verbatim; steps focus on *what the agent produces*, phases focused on *what the user experiences*.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:39:26.404424657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:24:35.320175039Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:24:35.317258733Z","close_reason":"Workflow section complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.16","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:39:26.404424657Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.16","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:13.345020662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.29","title":"Lighthouse / Core Web Vitals check","description":"Run Lighthouse against the article on a dev build + production build.

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.3","title":"Build jargon glossary (lib/wills-estate-jargon.ts)","description":"Create `lib/wills-estate-jargon.ts` modeled exactly on `lib/slack-migration-jargon.ts`: export a `getJargon(term: string)` function that returns a `{ term, definition, ... }` record.

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","title":"Record + embed install-flow screencast in article install section","description":"# Embedded screencast/GIF of full install flow in the article

## Why
Non-technical readers will read "paste a prompt into Claude Desktop, the agent installs the skill" and picture something alien. A 30-60 second silent video showing the full flow on screen is an order of magnitude more convincing. Existing software marketing pages universally use this pattern.

## Content
Silent screencast (no audio needed; the article is read in a browser tab).

Sequence to capture:
1. On jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill, cursor hovers the "Generate install prompt" button; button pressed; textarea appears with the prompt visibly populated + countdown "Expires in 10:00".
2. "Copy" button pressed; subtle confirmation.
3. Switch window to Claude Desktop (or Codex Desktop — can produce two variants and A/B which feels clearer).
4. Paste into the chat input; Enter.
5. Agent's tool-use confirmation dialog appears; "Allow" clicked.
6. Agent's streaming response: "Downloading... Extracting... Verifying..."
7. On-screen success: agent says "The skill is installed. Reload skills to see it in the picker."
8. Cmd-Shift-P → "Reload skills" → skill appears.
9. User types "/wills" → skill invokes → opening prompt appears.

Approximate 45-second runtime. No voiceover. Subtitles/captions overlay at key moments ("Generate code", "Paste into Claude", "Agent installs", "Done — skill available").

## Production
- Record on mac-mini-max in 1440p, downscale for web.
- Edit with the existing video tooling (see the obs-youtube-music skill if Jeffrey uses it).
- Export formats:
  - MP4 (H.264): mainline, broad browser support.
  - WebM (VP9): smaller; Chromium-family browsers will prefer.
  - GIF fallback: for email preview + contexts that won't play video.
- Poster image: a single high-quality frame showing the install card mid-flow.

## Placement in the article
- Inline inside the install section (hgjp.11), immediately after the description of Card 1 "In Claude or Codex Desktop (recommended, no terminal)".
- Use Next's <video> component with:
  - poster={...}
  - preload="metadata" (saves bytes; user clicks play to load the full thing)
  - controls
  - muted + playsInline (for autoplay-on-scroll behaviour if desired, which I'd keep off by default — autoplay is annoying)
  - Captions via <track kind="captions">; author SRT or WebVTT alongside.
- Lazy-load: the <video> element only fully loads when scrolled into viewport.

## Accessibility
- Captions required (WCAG SC 1.2.2).
- An expandable text description of the same flow immediately below the video (serves screen readers + anyone who skips video).
- Reduced-motion: keep autoplay disabled; respect prefers-reduced-motion by showing the poster as a static image.

## Storage
- Asset file at assets/install-screencast.mp4 etc.
- Served directly from Next's public/ (or via a CDN reference if too large for public/).
- Target file sizes: MP4 < 5 MB, WebM < 3 MB. Higher-quality variants available as optional downloads for anyone who wants them.

## Tests
- Playwright: assert the video element is present, poster loads, captions track is wired.
- Manual QA: play on Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Mobile Safari. Play on reduced-motion. Play with autoplay disabled in browser settings.

## Acceptance criteria
- Screencast recorded, edited, captioned.
- Embedded in hgjp.11 install section.
- Static poster + expandable text description both present.
- No regressions on Lighthouse (bd-3t7zd.29 — uh, hgjp.29) — the video should not delay LCP since it's below-the-fold.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:06.571263626Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T00:23:56.988622402Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:06.571263626Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:02.880431851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:02.767148100Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW round-3 2026-04-19: Video production + hosting strategy.
(1) Hosting: do not host a multi-MB video on the same origin as the article — hurts LCP budget and Vercel bandwidth bills. Use Cloudflare Stream (generous free tier) or Mux (best DX). Absolute worst case, use Vercel Blob and accept the cost. The article's <video> src points at the CDN URL.

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","title":"Interactive pricing calculator (attorney vs LegalZoom vs jsm)","description":"# Interactive pricing calculator inside the article

## Why
The static comparison block (hgjp.38) puts the dollar amounts on the page, but readers don't internalise them unless they see their own numbers. An interactive calculator makes it visceral. 

The goal is not precision — the goal is "holy crap, that's a lot of money I could save."

## Placement
Inline inside the article, near or inside hgjp.38 (comparison section). NOT a separate section with its own anchor — this is a visual embedded widget, small and scannable.

## Inputs (minimal — two rows)
- Net worth slider: $100k → $100M (log scale); defaults to $1M.
- Family situation (multi-select chips, 0+ selected):
  - Blended family
  - Minor children or dependents
  - Business or partnership interest
  - Vacation home / multi-state property
  - Vulnerable heir (addiction, disability, capacity concerns)
  - Non-citizen spouse
  - Crypto self-custody
  - Concentrated stock / pre-IPO

## Outputs (three lines, typeset large)
- Attorney consultation estimate: derived from net worth + complexity factor
  - Formula (deliberately rough): base $3000 + $0.00005 per $ of net worth above $1M + $2000 per complexity chip.
  - Capped at $40k for the "HNW with every complication" reader.
- LegalZoom / Trust & Will estimate: static $199-499 regardless of inputs (they don't scale with complexity; that's precisely the point).
- Jeffreys Skills.md: $20/month (cancel anytime).

Below the three figures, a small footnote: "Attorney estimates are indicative only; actual quotes vary widely by state and firm. Our estimate uses public billing-rate data (cite one or two sources) for transparency."

## UX details
- Updates live as the user drags or toggles.
- Savings tag: "Projected savings vs attorney consult: $N,NNN." — animated on every input change.
- Smooth transitions respect prefers-reduced-motion.
- Keyboard-accessible: slider supports arrow keys, chips are toggle buttons.
- A11y: aria-live on the output row so screen readers get updates.

## Implementation
Pure client-side React component in components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx or a sibling file. No server round-trips; every calculation is local.

## Honest framing
Add a one-line disclaimer: "This calculator is a rough guide, not a legal fee quote. Actual attorneys quote based on actual facts. Your real skill subscription is $20/month — the attorney number is so you understand the order of magnitude."

## Tests
- Slider moves across its full range; every position produces a plausible number (no NaN, no negative).
- All 8 chips toggle independently; outputs reflect the change.
- Analytics: pricing_calc_changed (net_worth_bucket, num_chips) fires on each input; gives us insight into what readers actually have.

## Acceptance criteria
- Widget renders in the comparison area.
- All interactions smooth + accessible.
- Disclaimer explicit.
- Analytics events wired.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:32.001688634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T00:23:57.084806847Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:32.001688634Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.38","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.312610349Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.206312451Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW round-3 2026-04-19: Pricing calc honesty + edge handling.
(1) Attorney rate data source citations: the formula's base + per-$ + per-chip coefficients must be derivable from public data (ABA billing-rate surveys, state bar reports). Add a sources paragraph below the calc: 'Rates derived from <ABA 2024 billing report>, <Martindale survey>, etc.' Without this the numbers feel pulled from thin air.
(2) Soft clamps: slider max is M; formula may produce silly numbers like k attorney for a k net worth (below the realistic -3k floor for a basic estate plan). Clamp outputs to a reasonable range (min k, max k for attorney; -499 for LegalZoom).
(3) Disclaimer proximity: the honest-framing sentence ('This calculator is a rough guide, not a legal fee quote') must be visually adjacent to the output numbers, not buried. Same font weight as the numbers themselves.
(4) No PII captured by the calc. Outputs derived client-side; analytics event records bucketed inputs (net_worth_bucket, num_chips) not raw values.
(5) Respect prefers-reduced-motion: slider live updates are animations; dampen them.
(6) Accessibility: slider is a native <input type='range'> with explicit min/max/step + aria-label; chips are toggle <button> with aria-pressed; outputs in aria-live='polite' region.","created_at":"2026-04-19T00:23:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.42","title":"Draft/preview mode for the article (pre-launch reviewer sharing)","description":"# Preview mode / share-token for pre-launch article

## Why
The legal / UPL review bead (hgjp.39), the content QA bead (hgjp.34), and the non-technical friend walkthrough (hgjp.34) all benefit from sharing a URL to the article BEFORE it ships. Also: Jeffrey might want one or two trusted reviewers (an attorney, a friend who read the tax article) to eyeball it before it hits Twitter.

Today, if we merge to main, Vercel publishes to production. There's no middle state.

## The design

### Option A: Vercel preview deployments (simplest)
Every PR or non-main branch auto-gets a preview URL from Vercel. Share that URL with reviewers. This already exists; the "new" thing is just using it deliberately.

Pro: zero code. Con: Vercel preview URLs are publicly accessible (no auth); anyone with the URL sees the article. For a launch article this is probably fine.

### Option B: Preview-token gate on the production site
Add a small middleware that checks ?preview=<token> query param OR a cookie. If the current article is within a "pre-publish" date window AND no valid token is present, serve a 404 (or a "launching soon" landing).

- Reserved tokens stored in a new small config in the repo (or in a database if jsm.md is doing this for all articles — but the personal-site is a static-first Next site, so a small table or env-backed list is fine).
- Tokens are opaque strings; minted on demand via a personal script.
- Token activates the article; without it the route is hidden.

### Recommended: Option A + small polish
Use Vercel preview deployments as the share mechanism. Add a lightweight `draft: true` frontmatter/flag to the article metadata; when draft, Next explicitly removes the article from /sitemap.xml, from the writing index (hgjp.5 registration), and from the command palette. When draft=false (publish day), the article surfaces everywhere.

This is the cleanest separation: draft state is a simple boolean in the article's metadata, reviewed in the content QA pass, flipped atomically at launch.

## Implementation

### Article metadata
```tsx
export const metadata = {
  title: "...",
  description: "...",
  alternates: { canonical: "/writing/wills-and-estate-planning" },
  robots: { index: false, follow: false }, // while draft
};

const DRAFT = true; // flip to false on launch day
```

### lib/content.ts
Skip draft articles when building the writing index:
```ts
if (!post.draft) writingList.push(post);
```

### sitemap.ts
Same: omit draft articles.

### command-palette.tsx
Same: omit draft articles from search index.

### Publishing
- Throughout development, draft=true; shareable via vercel-<branch>-<sha>.vercel.app URLs.
- Launch commit flips draft=false + robots: index, follow.

## Tests
- Playwright: in draft mode, /writing does not list the article; /sitemap.xml does not include it; Cmd-K "wills" returns no result. Yet direct visit to /writing/wills-and-estate-planning still renders (for authorized reviewers sharing the URL).
- Launch mode (draft=false): article appears in /writing, /sitemap.xml, command palette.

## Acceptance criteria
- draft flag implemented and wired to all three surfaces.
- Publishing is one commit: flip the flag.
- Search engines do not index draft articles (meta robots noindex).
- Tests cover both states.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:59.171182267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:34:58.884555586Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:34:58.881681355Z","close_reason":"Made draft-mode publication surfaces consistent for MDX-backed writing entries: writing index, sitemap, RSS, API search, and route metadata now honor draft visibility while direct preview URLs stay renderable.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.42","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:59.171182267Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.42","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW round-3 2026-04-19: Draft-mode safety belt.
(1) meta robots: while draft=true, the page emits <meta name='robots' content='noindex, nofollow'>. Verified via Playwright test that fetches the draft URL and asserts the header.
(2) Accidental index monitoring: add a simple once-daily cron that fetches the draft article URL, inspects the response for the noindex meta tag; if missing, page the operator. Catches the case where someone flips draft=false earlier than intended.
(3) The draft flag must be checked in THREE places per the bead description: sitemap.ts, lib/content.ts writing list, command-palette search index. Test all three — easy to miss one.
(4) Preview URL shareability: Vercel preview URLs are guessable enough that a reviewer who shares a screenshot can't inadvertently leak the URL via metadata. If that's a concern, add HTTP Basic auth to Vercel preview (Vercel Pro feature) or shrink the review window.
(5) Launch procedure: the commit that flips draft=false should ONLY flip that flag. Not bundled with other changes. Diff is one line; trivially reviewable.
(6) Post-launch: after the draft flag flips and the article is live, remove the robots-noindex cron check (it's no longer needed).","created_at":"2026-04-19T00:23:57Z"}]}

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","title":"Write agent-readable primer markdown (public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md)","description":"Write `public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md` — a single-file primer that a paying subscriber can download from the article and paste into any fresh Claude / Codex / ChatGPT session to boot the agent into the wills-estate-planning mindset.

## Purpose
This is the offline-friendly equivalent of the skill itself. The full skill is 201 files, ~24k lines, ~1 MB — too big to paste into a context window. The primer is a lossy but coherent summary the agent can use even without the full skill installed. Acts as:

1. **Proof-of-value tease** for visitors who are not yet paying subscribers (they can see the shape of the skill).
2. **Emergency fallback** for a paying subscriber whose agent setup isnt cooperating — they can paste this and still get useful output.
3. **The thing the "Download the operator primer as Markdown" button on the article points at** (see `PRIMER_HREF` in the article scaffold bead).

## Structure (draws from SKILL.md at ~/jeffreys_skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill/)
- `# Wills & Estate Planning — Operator Primer`
- TL;DR: what estate planning really is (coordination, not drafting), the 11 axioms condensed to the 5 most important ones, and the 2026 OBBBA $15M exemption reality.
- The kernel: axioms 0-11 in one-liner form with a sentence of why each matters.
- Wealth-tier routing (Tier 1-5 with one sentence each).
- The 9 intake phases as a numbered list.
- The 9-step workflow as a numbered list.
- The top 15 anti-patterns.
- The verification-first discipline (live-law checks).
- Output contract summary (intake/, analyses/, deliverables/).
- A short disclaimer: this primer is educational; an attorney signs the documents.
- Footer: "To get the full skill with all 17 subagents, 45 deliverable templates, and 135 reference files, subscribe at https://jeffreys-skills.md/ and install via the instructions at https://jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill"

## Format
- Plain Markdown.
- ~8-12 KB (smaller than slack primers 185 KB — this is deliberately a condensed version, not a complete operator guide).
- Semantic headings so an agent can index it.

## Acceptance criteria
- File exists at `public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md`.
- Articles download button successfully serves it as `application/octet-stream` with the configured filename.
- Content is self-contained: an agent with just this primer and no skill installed can still conduct a useful basic estate-planning intake.","notes":"test","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:51.386730372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:28:16.976876803Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:51.386730372Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"test comment","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:56:38Z"},{"id":2,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW 2026-04-18: Primer size target corrected from 8-12 KB to 30-60 KB (hard cap 200 KB). The primer is the **travelling alternative to the full skill** — if a non-subscriber pastes it into a fresh Claude/Codex/ChatGPT session, they should be able to conduct a meaningful 9-phase intake, name all 12 axioms + 14 operators, and produce a minimum-viable deliverable set (asset-inventory, beneficiary-map, red-flag-triage, letter-of-instruction, attorney-engagement-brief).  {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.8","title":"Download-the-primer CTA section","description":"Add the download-primer CTA directly under the hero, matching the slack article pattern (`MarkdownDownloadButton`).

## What it renders
A gradient-bordered pill containing a file icon + "Download the operator primer as Markdown" + sub-caption ("agent-readable · pairs with the full skill") + an `.md` badge.

Clicks download `public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md` as `WILLS_AND_ESTATE_PLANNING_PRIMER.md`.

```typescript
describe('Project Schema Validation', () => {
  test('all projects have required fields', () => {
    // Required: name, kind, href, short, description, tags
    projects.forEach(project => {
      expect(project.name).toBeDefined();
      expect(project.name.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
      expect(['product', 'oss', 'research']).toContain(project.kind);
      expect(project.href).toBeDefined();
      expect(project.short).toBeDefined();
      expect(project.description).toBeDefined();
      expect(Array.isArray(project.tags)).toBe(true);
      expect(project.tags.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2);
    });
  });

  test('optional fields have correct types when present', () => {
    // badge: string, size: enum, gradient: string
  });
});
```

### 2. URL Validation Tests
```typescript
describe('URL Validation', () => {
  test('all project hrefs are valid URLs', () => {
    projects.forEach(project => {
      expect(() => new URL(project.href)).not.toThrow();
      expect(project.href).toMatch(/^https?:\/\//);
    });
  });

  test('no duplicate hrefs exist', () => {
    const hrefs = projects.map(p => p.href);
    const uniqueHrefs = new Set(hrefs);
    expect(uniqueHrefs.size).toBe(hrefs.length);
  });

  test('GitHub URLs use correct format', () => {
    // https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/<repo>
  });
});
```

### 3. Content Quality Tests
```typescript
describe('Content Quality', () => {
  test('short descriptions are actually short (< 150 chars)', () => {
    projects.forEach(project => {
      expect(project.short.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(150);
      expect(project.short.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(20);
    });

  test('descriptions are substantive (50-500 chars)', () => {
    projects.forEach(project => {
      expect(project.description.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(50);
      expect(project.description.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(500);
    });
  });

  test('no duplicate project names', () => {
    const names = projects.map(p => p.name.toLowerCase());
    const uniqueNames = new Set(names);
    expect(uniqueNames.size).toBe(names.length);
  });
});
```

### 4. Badge Consistency Tests
```typescript
describe('Badge Consistency', () => {
  test('star badges follow consistent format', () => {
    // Format: "123 stars" or "1.2K stars" or "2.8K stars"
    const starBadgePattern = /^(\d+\.?\d*K?)\s+stars$/;
    projects.forEach(project => {
      if (project.badge && project.badge.includes('star')) {
        expect(project.badge).toMatch(starBadgePattern);
      }
    });
  });
});
```

  });

});
